*** 1 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND 90 MPH MICROBURST DAMAGE ALONG HIGHWAY 242 EAST OF BLADENBORO. THE PATH WIDTH WAS 370 YARDS AND PATH LENGTH WAS 0.7 MILES. IT WAS ESTIMATED THAT THE MICROBURST CAUSED DAMAGE UNTIL 710 PM EDT. THERE WAS 1 MINOR INJURY WHEN A TREE FELL ON A MOBILE HOME CAUSING MODERATE DAMAGE. A METAL CAR SHOP ALSO SUFFERED MINOR TO MODERATE DAMAGE...WHILE A POORLY BUILT MOBILE HOME ON THE NORTH SIDE OF HIGHWAY 242 WAS DESTROYED. 40+ TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED.

LARGE TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN. SOME TREES SNAPPED HALFWAY UP. A FEW STRUCTURES LOST SHINGLES. LENGTH OF THE DAMAGE...15O YARDS. WIDTH...50 YARDS. EMERGENCY MANAGER AT THE SITE DETERMINED DAMAGE TO BE DUE TO STRAIGHT LINE WINDS.
SEVERAL REPORTS OF DAMAGE IN THE VICINITY OF TV ROAD BETWEEN MCIVER ROAD AND I-95...INCLUDING A PARKED 18-WHEELER ROLLED OVER...AND TREE DOWN ON 2 MOBILE HOMES.
NEW HANOVER COUNTY 911 CENTER REPORTS THAT THE BOTTOM FLOORS OF APARTMENT COMPLEXES SUCH AS CAMPUS EDGE AND BROOKSTONE ON RACINE DRIVE ARE FLOODED.

NEW HANOVER CTY 911 REPORTS TWO INSTANCES OF A VEHICLE PUSHED BY FLOOD WATERS INTO A RETENTION POND. ONE IN THE SAMS CLUB PARKING LOT ON COLLEGE ROAD AND ANOTHER IN THE FROG POND PLACE APARTMENT COMPLEX ON DARLINGTON AVE.
A CANAL HAS OVERFLOWED ITS BANKS AND ONTO PRINCESS PLACE DRIVE BETWEEN BARCLAY HILLS DRIVE AND NORTH 23RD STREET. ONE FOOT OF WATER WAS OBSERVED FLOWING ACROSS THE ROADWAY.
CAROLINA BEACH LIFEGUARDS REPORTED THAT A WATERSPOUT DEVELOPED 3 MILES EAST OF CAROLINA BEACH PIER AT 1125 AM EDT. THE WATERSPOUT DISSIPATED AT 1132 AM EDT.
HIGHWAY 17 IS CLOSED IN GEORGETOWN DUE TO SIGNIFICANT FLOODING. MANY MANY REPORTS OF FLOODING IN STREETS AND IN YARDS AROUND THE CITY. SOME VEHICLES ARE INUNDATED.
PORTIONS OF U.S. HIGHWAY 17/FRAZIER STREET CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING IN GEORGETOWN. OTHER MAJOR STREETS CLOSED INCLUDE HAZARD STREET...CONGDON STREET...AND U.S. HIGHWAY 521/HIGHMARKET STREET.
TEN PEOPLE STRANDED IN CITY HALL WERE RESCUED ON A JON BOAT. SIX PEOPLE WERE RESCUED FROM A FLOODED CAR AT THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHMARKET AND CANNON STREET. TIME ESTIMATED.
